commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server event-projector guard that suppresses one unphased raw assistant item when a typed commentary echo is pending, plus regression coverage that preserves a later unphased raw note.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +69. Total +78 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main can store an unphased Codex raw assistant item and later resolve it as visible assistant output because only `phase === "commentary"` raw echoes consume the pending commentary counter. I did not run a live WebChat reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of unit-only proof, not a concrete automated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): suppress raw commentary echo when phase field is absent
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: In WebChat with Codex-backed providers, internal commentary text (e.g. "commentary to=functions.browser") leaks into user-visible chat bubbles. The Codex app-server can ship raw response echoes for commentary items WITHOUT a `phase` field, and the existing guard at `event-projector.ts:957` only matches `phase === "commentary"` — unphased raw items fell through and were stored as visible assistant text.

**Solution**: Add a 2-line fallback guard in `handleRawResponseItemCompleted`: when `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0` but the raw item has no `phase` field, consume the counter and suppress the item, matching the existing 1:1 deduplication contract between typed commentary completions and raw response echoes.

**What changed**: 
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts`: +9 (6-line guard + 3-line comment at line 963)
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`: +69 (dedicated test at line 1450)

Fixes #89668

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Raw commentary echoes shipped without a `phase` field by the Codex app-server leak into visible WebChat replies. After fix, the unphased echo is consumed by the counter and the later distinct raw-only note is delivered correctly.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-89668` at `813498590d`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` + direct source inspection of guard code

### Proof A — Code structure verification

Direct inspection of the PR head source confirms the guard at `event-projector.ts:963-968`:
```
// FIX #89668: The Codex app-server may ship raw response echoes for
// commentary items without a phase field.  When a typed commentary
// completion was already echoed, conservatively suppress the paired
// raw item even when its phase is absent, so the commentary text
// cannot leak into visible assistant output.
if (!phase && this.pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0) {
  this.pendingRawCommentaryEchoes -= 1;
  return;
}
```

- Guard present ✅
- Correct guard check: `(!phase && pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0)` ✅
- Counter consumed: `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes -= 1` ✅
- Returns immediately ✅

### Proof B — Full test suite (99/99 passed)

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  99 passed (99)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 28.17s
```

### Proof C — Dedicated regression test

The new test "suppresses a raw commentary echo whose phase field is missing from the envelope" at line 1450 exercises the exact scenario:

1. Typed commentary completion → `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes = 1`, preamble emitted
2. Raw response echo WITHOUT `phase` → consumed by new guard, counter → 0
3. Later distinct raw-only note (also no phase) → counter is 0, stored normally
4. Turn completed → `buildResult().assistantTexts` contains "Still working"

Two assertions:
- `preambles`: only "Checking the workspace" (typed echo) — raw echo suppressed ✅
- `assistantTexts`: contains "Still working" — later note delivered via buildResult ✅

**Observed result after the fix**:

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Raw echo WITH `phase: "commentary"` | Suppressed ✅ | Suppressed ✅ |
| Raw echo WITHOUT `phase` | **Leaked into visible text** ❌ | Suppressed ✅ |
| Distinct raw-only note (no phase) | Delivered ✅ | Delivered ✅ |

**What was not tested**: Live WebChat with Codex + browser tool against a real app-server endpoint. This requires a functional Codex provider with valid API credentials and active model producing browser tool calls with commentary text. The fix operates at the event-projection layer — a deterministic counter-mismatch between two parallel event lanes (typed commentary completions and raw response echoes). The counter mechanism is entirely local to the projector instance and does not depend on external network state.

**Evidence provenance**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` + `node --import tsx/esm` source inspection on the PR head at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-25.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ 99/99 | event-projector.test.ts |
| Guard Code | ✅ | 2-line guard verified at event-projector.ts:963-968 |
| buildResult | ✅ | assistantTexts contains "Still working" after turn completed |

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area?** The conservative suppression guard could consume a raw item that is NOT a commentary echo if the counter was incremented by a different mechanism.

**Risk level**: Low — `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes` is only incremented by `handleItemCompleted` at the typed commentary echo path (line 694: `isCommentaryAssistantItem` check). Each increment has a 1:1 correspondence with a raw echo. The guard is after the `phase === "commentary"` check, so it only handles the no-phase edge case.

**Mitigation**: Counter-based approach ensures at most one suppression per typed echo. If the app-server sends multiple unphased raws for one commentary, only the first is consumed — rest fall through to visible text (fail-safe).

**Merge risk declaration**: `message-delivery` — suppresses raw response items that were previously leaking. Changes what the user sees (removes garbage), not what the model produces.
